grandCentral.ipBlockedLead: This admin area is not accessible from your current network address. If you are an administrator, please log in from an authorized IP (network specified in the configuration).
grandCentral.ipBlockedHint: No action is required on your part if you are not intended for this access.
grandCentral.ipBlockedApiMessage: The admin API is not accessible from this network address.
grandCentral.intrusionEmailSubject: 🛡️ Grand Central — Attempted or Denied Access

councilDigest.msgSentinel: Degraded Lines: {deg}. Critical: {crit}. Detail: Sentinel panel / system_incidents.
councilDigest.msgBabelEmpty: No daily metrics yet — initiate a Forge snapshot or wait for the weekly cron.
councilDigest.msgNexus: Queue and diagnostics in Nexus Live — human validation required for sensitive actions on files.
councilDigest.consensusGuardianReview: Compliance: Guardian draft pending — check email + Compliance panel.
councilDigest.consensusRas: No urgent issues: ongoing monitoring; no auto-decision.

babelForgeMutator.priorityLine: Priority messages: name personalization + Google rating, a quantified proof, a single CTA, strict local language.

broadcastTranslate.errorNoAi: No AI key to translate emails.
broadcastTranslate.systemPrompt: You are a professional translator for the REPUTEXA SaaS platform (online reputation). Strict JSON only.
broadcastTranslate.lineInstructions: Translate the subject and body to: {targets}. Maintain HTML tags and URLs. Natural local adaptation (no heavy literal translation).

complianceSentinel.title: Compliance Agent
complianceSentinel.introRich: A monitoring assistant keeps an eye on useful news (GDPR, data rules, signals around online reviews, consumer topics). When something important changes, it can prepare a draft text — always to be reviewed by a human before publication.
complianceSentinel.bulletPersonalDataRich: Personal Data — new rules or major reminders from authorities when they appear in monitoring.
complianceSentinel.bulletGoogleReviewsRich: Google Listings / Reviews — what affects how to collect or display reviews when sources discuss them.
complianceSentinel.bulletTransparencyRich: Client Information and Transparency — themes like clear pricing, fine print, loyalty when monitoring identifies them.
complianceSentinel.pendingDocsLine: {count, plural, =0 {no suggestions in queue} one {# suggested modification} other {# suggested modifications}}
complianceSentinel.emailFallbackRich: When a draft is ready, the admin email includes a summary + text preview (double AI review from Claude + GPT-4o) — it publishes nothing without your approval. Also check spam / correct inbox (ADMIN_COMPLIANCE_EMAIL or admin profile).
complianceSentinel.guideTitle: Simple Explanations — Compliance Agent & Legal Guardian
complianceSentinel.guideWhatRich: Guardian reviews useful news and can propose drafts of Terms of Service, privacy policy, or legal mentions. You retain the final legal say: nothing goes online without human validation.
complianceSentinel.guideColorRich: Green: everything was fine at the last check. Orange: nothing has turned yet or there are drafts to handle. Red: the last round failed. This is not a lawyer: a real legal expert is still recommended for major issues.
complianceSentinel.guideOrderStep1: Check this dashboard and your emails when a draft is ready.
complianceSentinel.guideOrderStep2Rich: Open Compliance: review the queue, open each draft, check what your internal team requests.
complianceSentinel.guideOrderStep3Rich: Publish Legal Text: import the draft if needed, fill in summary and date (with legal deadline), preview, then publish — users are notified at the right time.
complianceSentinel.guideOrderStep4: Use the Audit Kit if an inspector wants clean evidence.
complianceSentinel.guideLinksItem1Rich: Open Compliance: the page with drafts and log.
complianceSentinel.guideLinksItem2Rich: Publish Legal Text: jump to the form lower on this same admin page.
complianceSentinel.guideMailsP: A recap can be sent when a draft is created. Check spam and ensure the correct admin address is noted as compliance contact.

codeGuardian.introRich: The code mechanic: it inspects dirt, brakes, and large shaky parts, without writing anything on its own in Git. You copy the list or send it to the team to fix on your branch.
codeGuardian.guideTitle: Simple Explanations — Code Guardian · Asset Health
codeGuardian.guideIdeaRich: Code Guardian performs a read-only technical inspection: organization, possible slowdowns, major structural issues. Nothing goes to Git on its own: it’s up to you to copy, paste, or open a branch.
codeGuardian.guideScoreP: It summarizes the last complete pass, not 'is the site online' (that’s elsewhere). A score that drops after a major delivery often happens; focus on the trend and urgent points marked.
codeGuardian.guideAuditBtnRich: Launch the improvement audit asks the server to take another tour of the property. You get a list by theme and a text file ready to paste into Jira, Linear, or a PR.
codeGuardian.guideBenchRich: Paste an excerpt + two lines of context: quick feedback for a review, without executing the code and without reading your secrets.
codeGuardian.guideHabitP: A scan after major refactor or before production deployment; you handle first what screams 'urgent', then the rest. Keep the export in your tracking tool to see if it improves over the weeks.
codeGuardian.noScanYet: No scan yet — launch a first audit.
codeGuardian.findingsShownLine: {shown, plural, one {# point displayed} other {# points displayed}} out of {total}
codeGuardian.filterNoMatch: No points for this filter — choose 'All' or another theme.
codeGuardian.labIntro: Select a card above to view the fix — copy it into a dedicated branch. No automatic patch on the server.
codeGuardian.supremeIntro: Quick automatic review on a piece of code you paste (common topics: security, Stripe, Tailwind). It does not replace real tests or a developer reviewing.
codeGuardian.draftDismissConfirm: Dismiss this draft? You can relaunch monitoring in the next cycle.
codeGuardian.draftVerifyLabel: I certify I have reviewed sources + draft
codeGuardian.draftVerifiedLine: Verified on {date} — you can import the draft into 'Publish a new version'.
codeGuardian.cleanExportHeaderDisclaimer: # No automatic modification of the repository — apply in Cursor / PR review.

consoleNoise.title: Noisy console: ~{total} log/debug/info/trace call(s)
consoleNoise.suggestedFix: In production, prefer a conditional logger (if (process.env.NODE_ENV !== "production")) or remove traces. Do not commit console.log in critical API paths.

importHeavy.title: {fileCount} file(s) with many imports (≥ {threshold})
importHeavy.suggestedFix: Perform a manual sort: group imports from the same module, remove dead imports via ESLint (`unused-imports`) or the IDE's action palette. Do not delete blindly without review.

todoMarkers.title: Scattered TODO/FIXME markers ({fileCount}+ affected files)
todoMarkers.detail: Debt tracking: prioritize a backlog pass or tickets.
todoMarkers.suggestedFix: Convert TODOs into tracked issues; remove obsolete FIXMEs after review.

selectNoLimit.title: Wide selection possible without .limit()
selectNoLimit.suggestedFix: In {file}, add .limit(N) or pagination, and SQL indexes on filtered columns (e.g., user_id, created_at). For stable aggregates, consider a short cache (5 min) via unstable_cache or materialized table as appropriate.

hugeComponent.title: Large component (~{lines} lines)
hugeComponent.suggestedFix: Break down {file}: extract UI sub-blocks into components in the same folder (e.g., *Section.tsx), isolate logic into a hook use*.ts, keep the root file under ~200 lines. Check props and avoid circular dependencies.

sampleClean.title: Sample without strong signal
sampleClean.detail: {filesScanned} files scanned — widen depth or connect ESLint/TS strict in CI for further insights.
sampleClean.suggestedFix: Maintain this monthly audit rhythm + human review before any merging.

codeGuardianOperational.checkNoEvalDetailFail: Dangerous patterns detected — reject or isolate.
codeGuardianOperational.checkNoChildLabel: No ad hoc shell execution in the snippet
codeGuardianOperational.checkStripeLabel: Logical Stripe / billing markers still present
codeGuardianOperational.checkStripeDetailFail: The context mentions Stripe — confirm that nothing essential has been removed.
codeGuardianOperational.checkTailwindDetailFail: Check that the Tailwind design has not been inadvertently emptied.
codeGuardianOperational.checkTailwindNaLabel: Tailwind design impact (not required by context)
codeGuardianOperational.checkDhtmlDetailFail: Require documented sanitization if raw HTML.
codeGuardianOperational.checkPerfDetailFail: Consider .limit(), short cache, or SQL index as appropriate.
codeGuardianOperational.summaryOk: Static checks OK. Code to be applied manually in Cursor after tests and peer review if needed.
codeGuardianOperational.summaryReview: Blocking or doubtful points — correct before merging.

internalHealth.webhooksNoPublicUrl: No public URL: add SENTINEL_PUBLIC_URL (prod URL for webhooks), or NEXT_PUBLIC_SITE_URL / NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL in .env.local. On Vercel, VERCEL_URL is used if nothing else is defined.

productAiContext.promptInstructions: The 'REPOSITORY CONTEXT' block below lists recent commits, affected files, and a CHANGELOG excerpt: use it to accurately name delivered features or affected product areas. Do not create invented technical details absent from this block or the text provided by the user. If the block is off-topic, ignore it.

complianceBundlePdf.coverBody: This PDF compiles: list of subcontractors (public), template of logs-locations.md (public), processing register (Art.30 tables), journal and frozen archives of the operator file for the selected month (UTC), status of boxes at generation. It does not replace signed DPAs or legal advice. To be kept outside Git (Drive or internal binder).
complianceBundlePdf.section1Title: 1. List of Subcontractors (public file at the time of generation)
complianceBundlePdf.section2Title: 2. Logs Locations Template (logs-locations.md public — do not version once filled)
complianceBundlePdf.regIntro: Each processing occupies one or more pages: labels in emphasis, aligned values, alternating lines for reading in due diligence file.
complianceBundlePdf.registerParseFallback: [Parse HTML register: structure not recognized — fallback to plain text.]
complianceBundlePdf.logEmpty: No events recorded during this period (comparison timezone: UTC).
complianceBundlePdf.snapshotsEmpty: No frozen archive during this period.
complianceBundlePdf.section6Title: 6. Boxes checked at the time of generation (snapshot, all periods)
complianceBundlePdf.stateLineTasks: {done} / {total} tasks marked as done in the operator file.
complianceBundlePdf.section7Title: 7. Commitment / Signature (to be completed by hand or digitally)
complianceBundlePdf.sigLine1: I certify to have supervised the operational compliance covered by this file for the indicated period,
complianceBundlePdf.sigLine2: within the limits of the disclaimer on the kit page (no substitution for legal advice).

ghostProtocol.logScanMessage: Ghost Protocol — {label} ({count, plural, one {# observation} other {# observations}})

findings.dastAuthDeniedDetail: Reduces IDOR risk on account data.
findings.sastCleanTitle: No obvious reporting (eval / new Function / innerHTML) on the sample
findings.sastCleanDetail: {count} files scanned under app/api and lib.

securityRandomAudit.injContactJsonOkDetail: Reduces surface; fields remain typed on multipart only.
securityRandomAudit.injReportValidateTitle: Report-issue: rejects or limits malformed payloads / throughput
securityRandomAudit.injReportValidateDetail: HTTP {status} — no visible 500 on synthetic load.
securityRandomAudit.injReportAcceptTitle: Report-issue accepted a body with XSS/SQL patterns in text
securityRandomAudit.injReportAcceptDetail: May be normal if neutralized server-side only — check escapeHtml and absence of raw HTML rendering.
securityRandomAudit.injVerifyOtpDetail: Reduces empty requests against OTP verification.
securityRandomAudit.permNoSessionDetail: Expected behavior to reduce surface theft / credits.
securityRandomAudit.permOpen200Detail: Immediately correct access control on this route.
securityRandomAudit.permOtherDetail: Check if maintenance, WAF, or nonexistent route.
securityRandomAudit.depOsvErrorDetail: Retry later; keep npm audit in CI.
securityRandomAudit.depOsvReportsDetail: Open https://osv.dev and plan upgrade / mitigation.
securityRandomAudit.depOsvOkTitle: No OSV notice listed for {name} (current version)
securityRandomAudit.depOsvOkDetail: Stay on npm audit monitoring in CI.
securityRandomAudit.cfgNoBaseTitle: Public URL absent — headers not tested
securityRandomAudit.cfgFetchDetail: Header checks impossible on this execution.
securityRandomAudit.cfgNoHstsDetail: Recommended in production (reverse proxy or next.config headers).
securityRandomAudit.cfgNoCspDetail: Consider CSP report-only then gradual strengthening.
securityRandomAudit.vpInjectionDescription: Add .min/.max bounds, NFKC Unicode normalization, and reject typical XSS/SQL patterns in input before any processing.
securityRandomAudit.vpPermissionsTargetHint: middleware.ts or createClient + getUser on each /api/admin/*
securityRandomAudit.vpPermissionsDescription: Ensure all admin routes call getUser() and role === admin before any logic; returns 401/403 unified.
securityRandomAudit.vpDependenciesDescription: Apply the corrected version indicated by npm audit / OSV, then E2E tests and changelog review (breaking changes).
securityRandomAudit.vpConfigDescription: Enable HSTS, CSP report-only then enforce, Referrer-Policy; remove any secret from NEXT_PUBLIC_* keys.
securityRandomAudit.vpConfigSnippet:
// Example headers next.config
{ key: 'Strict-Transport-Security', value: 'max-age=63072000; includeSubDomains; preload' }
securityRandomAudit.auditLogMessage: Red Team auto — axis {axis}, {findingsCount} finding(s), {patchesCount} virtual patch(es)

securityPerfectionAudit.noPublicBaseDetail: Set NEXT_PUBLIC_SITE_URL or SENTINEL_PUBLIC_URL to enable remote HTTP probes (401 / 400).
securityPerfectionAudit.adminAuthShellTitle: Admin route without session: access denied (expected)
securityPerfectionAudit.adminAuthShellDetail: Simulating 'AI credit theft': an unauthenticated client cannot call the admin health-check. Follow up with a review of AI provider routes server-side (keys never exposed to the browser).
securityPerfectionAudit.adminAuthShellJournal: Attempt to call admin API without token → 401/403 (expected behavior).
securityPerfectionAudit.adminExposedTitle: Alert: admin health-check accessible without auth
securityPerfectionAudit.adminExposedDetail: The /api/admin/health-check route responded 200 without cookie — check deployment and middleware.
securityPerfectionAudit.adminExposedJournal: API simulation: admin endpoint responds 200 without session (!)
securityPerfectionAudit.adminProbeInconclusiveDetail: HTTP response {status} — check network, CDN, or maintenance.
securityPerfectionAudit.contactRejectsJsonTitle: Contact form: JSON body rejected (multipart expected)
securityPerfectionAudit.contactRejectsJsonDetail: Injection simulation: a malicious JSON body does not pass the multipart entry — surface reduction. Data remains processed via typed fields server-side.
securityPerfectionAudit.contactUnexpectedTitle: Unexpected behavior contact for JSON body
securityPerfectionAudit.reportIssueValidateDetail: No execution on minimal body — 'invalid data' simulation confirmed.
securityPerfectionAudit.bruteforcePolicyDetail: No massive password attempts: this would saturate Supabase Auth, risk lock-out, and violate best practices. Check in the Supabase dashboard: rate limits, captcha / email OTP, MFA for REPUTEXA admin accounts.
securityPerfectionAudit.bruteforcePolicyJournal: Bruteforce simulation: skip network — review Auth configuration and MFA recommended.
securityPerfectionAudit.cronSecretMissingDetail: Scheduled jobs can be invoked without a strong secret — to be corrected in production.
securityPerfectionAudit.cronOkDetail: Cron jobs protected by expected Bearer header.
securityPerfectionAudit.adminSecretMissingDetail: Some legal actions / publish may depend on this secret.
securityPerfectionAudit.uxRumDetail: Gains like '1.2s → 0.3s' require field measurements (Vercel Analytics, Web Vitals, Supabase traces). This module records a recommendation; connect a RUM for actionable alerts.
securityPerfectionAudit.uxRumJournal: Peer advice: enable RUM to transform '30% click elsewhere' into real heatmaps.
securityPerfectionAudit.godModePeerDetail: The 'God Mode' (v1) never writes to the Git repository: no automatic changes to pricing or design. Use the kill switch to immediately cut off planned automated actions in upcoming versions.
securityPerfectionAudit.syntheticPeerMessage: Peer: keep the kill switch handy — AI should not modify code, pricing, or charter without human validation.
securityPerfectionAudit.peerTip: In production, complement with a bug bounty, dependency scan (npm audit, Snyk), and OWASP ASVS review; this panel remains an internal awareness net.

sentinel360Audit.legalMissingDocDetail: Missing: {missing}. Publish all three types for compliance coverage.
sentinel360Audit.legalAiGapTitle: AI features without clear mention identified
sentinel360Audit.legalAiGapDetail: OpenAI / Anthropic seems configured server-side, but published texts do not clearly mention 'artificial intelligence', providers, or limits — align Terms of Service / privacy policy.
sentinel360Audit.legalWhatsappGapTitle: Active WhatsApp channel without clear mention
sentinel360Audit.legalWhatsappGapDetail: A WhatsApp / Meta token is present in configuration. Ensure privacy policy and Terms of Service describe the processing of messages / metadata.
sentinel360Audit.legalCookieMentionDetail: No standard wording on cookies / consent / ePrivacy detected in the three documents — check policy and banner.
sentinel360Audit.legalSiretTitle: SIRET number not detected in legal mentions
sentinel360Audit.legalSiretDetail: The SIRET pattern (14 digits) is absent from published mentions. Complete if you are subject to it (France).
sentinel360Audit.guardianStaleDetail: Last Guardian cycle: {days} days ago. A relaunch reduces regulatory risk.
sentinel360Audit.guardianNeverDetail: Schedule /api/cron/legal-guardian or trigger monitoring from this panel.
sentinel360Audit.consentsSparseTitle: Few or no cookie consents recorded (30 days)
sentinel360Audit.consentsSparseDetail: The user_consents register is empty in the recent window. Check the banner and visitor journey.
sentinel360Audit.publicUrlDetail: Set NEXT_PUBLIC_SITE_URL or SENTINEL_PUBLIC_URL to automatically test legal pages.
sentinel360Audit.linksOkDetail: HEAD check on main EN paths.

growthEquirectMap.emptyHint: No geolocated points. Launch the sniper (Places) or provide lat/lng on client properties.

babelWizard.errInvalidLocaleCode: Invalid language code (e.g., it, pt, nl).
babelWizard.errInvalidBackupFile: Invalid file (not a Babel Wizard backup).
babelWizard.msgImportedFile: State imported from file. You can save to the server to continue later.
babelWizard.msgPreviewGenerated: Preview generated — review then check validation.
babelWizard.msgMessagesAllDone: messages/*.json: all batches are completed. Review then validate.
babelWizard.errSafetyLimit: Safety limit reached — continue manually batch by batch.
babelWizard.errValidateStepFirst: Validate the step (check box) before continuing.
babelWizard.confirmReset: Reset the assistant on this screen? (the server session remains in the database if already created.)
babelWizard.checkpointBeforeRich: To be able to 'go back' in the code, use Git (commit or branch). The buttons above only save the state of this assistant (generated texts, current step) — handy if the browser crashes or to continue tomorrow.
babelWizard.checkpointSteps: 1) Download a backup (even empty) with the button above. 2) Enter the language. 3) Move to the next step: server backup is automatic when you advance (you can also 'Save server + file' now).
babelWizard.authCheckbox: I have verified the AuthEmails keys in messages/{localeCode}.json
babelWizard.warCheckbox: It's good for publication / no gate on this locale
babelWizard.emailsCheckbox: I noted what remains to be audited manually
babelWizard.singulariteBodyRich: One click: JSON bundle + email stubs + configs, anchored smart-merge (<rich>@babel-anchor</rich>), forced git commit, HTTP probe on <rich>/{localeCode}</rich>, <rich>tmp/babel-wizard-dev-restart-requested.txt</rich> marker. The dev server cannot be restarted from the API: restart <rich>npm run dev</rich> yourself to validate routes and Prisma.
babelWizard.advancedOptionsBodyRich: <rich>BABEL_FILESYSTEM_WRITE_ENABLED=true</rich> required. Smart-merge reads tmp/babel-wizard-apply/{pathFragment}/. Commit: <rich>BABEL_GIT_COMMIT_ENABLED=true</rich>. Then <rich>npm run build</rich> or dev restart.
babelWizard.optSmartMerge: Include smart-merge (snippets → catalog, email pack, signup, SEO, stubs)
babelWizard.optGitCommit: Git commit after writing (only affected files)
babelWizard.diskWriteBlurb: Writes <rich>messages/*.json</rich>, <rich>tmp/…</rich>, then optionally merges TS and commits.
babelWizard.noPreview: — No preview — generate with AI or complete the previous step.
babelWizard.previewCheckbox: I have reviewed: content OK for integration into code
babelWizard.staticAuthBody:
After the 'Catalog' step, the locale is in SITE_LOCALE_CODES: normalizeEmailLocale() in lib/emails/auth-email-i18n.ts already accepts it.

Check that messages/{localeCode}.json contains the AuthEmails / zenith keys (same structure as fr.json) so that activation emails do not revert to French due to missing keys.
babelWizard.staticWarBody:
If you set a gateCountryCode on this locale in site-locales-catalog.ts, open the Growth War Room and activate the country + 'public site' for /{localeCode} to be visible.

Otherwise, this step is already OK.
babelWizard.staticEmailsBody:
Open lib/emails/templates.ts and lib/emails/info-broadcast-email.ts: search for hardcoded strings or languages and align them with the catalog (SITE_LOCALE_CODES) or on profiles.language.

This part remains manual until a complete audit of the repository.
babelWizard.stepMeta_checkpoint_short: Restoration point before starting, then entering the language.
babelWizard.stepMeta_messages_short: Complete next-intl file (transcreation by batches, same engine as Expansion).

sentinel.overallHealing: Agent Claude 3.5 healing in progress ({count, plural, one {# service} other {# services}})...
sentinel.reportFooterPaste: Paste this report into your ticketing or AI support tool
sentinel.guideTitle: Sentinel Guide — buttons and reading indicators
sentinel.guideLi1Rich: Refresh immediately restarts health-check + incidents; otherwise, auto-refresh every 30s.
sentinel.guideLi2Rich: AI Auto-repair (visible if critical) calls the auto-heal endpoint: diagnostics and proposed actions server-side, not a magic deployment.
sentinel.guideLi3Rich: Copy Diagnosis exports a text report (services, latencies, last incidents) for internal sharing.
sentinel.guideLi4Rich: Indicators green / amber / red by service: timeout or network error → often amber or red even if the provider is healthy (check API keys and firewalls).
sentinel.guideLi5Rich: The top banner of the admin panel summarizes the same health with Vault and Guardian.
sentinel.healTitleRunning: Analysis in progress via Claude 3.5 Sonnet (Private Agent)...
sentinel.healTitleIdle: Trigger Sentinel auto-repair with Claude 3.5 Sonnet (Private Agent)
sentinel.copyTitle: Copy report for analysis by Claude 3.5 Sonnet (Private Agent).
sentinel.loadingTitle: Sentinel Health Check — webhooks tested via resolved public URL (SENTINEL_PUBLIC_URL, NEXT_PUBLIC_SITE_URL or VERCEL_URL)
sentinel.loadingHint: Checking webhooks via public URL (production or Vercel). Help Claude 3.5 Sonnet (Private Agent): report 'Sentinel AI Diagnosis'.
sentinel.backupBody: Sentinel does not automatically drop a nightly copy on S3, Drive, or other third-party storage. To survive a major DB provider outage, enable Supabase backup options (PITR, scheduled exports) and maintain a replicated copy per your internal policy (snapshot to S3 bucket, etc.).
sentinel.metaLine: Auto-refresh 30s · Cron Sentinel 10min · Agent Claude automatic triggering

sentinel360.triggerTitle: 360° Flash Audit — legal, GDPR, consistency, payment
sentinel360.modalIntro: Scanner type 'total compliance': legal texts vs actual functions, cookie register & Guardian, critical links, Stripe alignment. Automatic fixes remain limited to safe actions (e.g., relaunch Guardian).
sentinel360.fortressIntroRich: Indicators for this session. Only Active or Inactive — no secrets or addresses displayed.
sentinel360.ipFilteringHint: Admin network wall if the server-side list is set.
sentinel360.browserBindHint: Binding cookie present in this browser (server secret required).
sentinel360.ipWarningRich: In case of change of residence, operator, or phone number, remember to update the <rich>ADMIN_ALLOWED_IPS</rich> variable in your Vercel dashboard with your new IP address, as well as your phone number in your profile or relevant settings, to maintain access.
